from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about artists (or related entities) into a fluent description."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(value: str) -> str:
        """Strip surrounding quotes and whitespace."""
        return value.strip().strip('"').strip("'")

    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language fragments.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the cleaned object string.
    pred_map = {
        "birthDate": "was born on {obj}",
        "birthYear": "was born in {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        "genre": "has a genre of {obj}",
        "instrument": "plays the {obj}",
        "occupation": "is a {obj}",
        "nationality": "is {obj}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "is associated with {obj}",
        "recordLabel": "is signed to {obj}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "started performing in {obj}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": "ended performing in {obj}",
        "deathDate": "died on {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "alternativeName": "is also known as {obj}",
        "stylisticOrigin": "has its stylistic origins in {obj}",
        "musicFusionGenre": "has a fusion genre of {obj}",
        "musicSubgenre": "has a subgenre {obj}",
        "derivative": "has a derivative genre {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        # leader is handled specially to place the object as the subject.
        "leader": "is led by {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {obj}",
        "parentCompany": "is owned by {obj}",
        "bandMember": "has band member {obj}",
        "capital": "has capital {obj}",
        "anthem": "has anthem {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the currency {obj}",
        "demonym": "has demonym {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "distributingCompany": "is distributed by {obj}",
        "training": "was trained at {obj}",
        "populationDensity": "has a population density of {obj}",
        "meaning": "means \"{obj}\"",
        "longName": "full name is \"{obj}\"",
        "areaTotal": "covers an area of {obj}",
        "foundingDate": "was founded on {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "lies at {obj} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
        "language": "speaks {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "has official language {obj}",
        "background": "has a background as {obj}",
    }

    # Group clauses by their subject to allow multiple subjects in the input.
    # Aggregate objects per subject and predicate to allow natural list joining.
    def _join_items(items: list[str]) -> str:
        """Join a list of strings with commas and 'and' before the last item."""
        if not items:
            return ""
        if len(items) == 1:
            return items[0]
        return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + " and " + items[-1]

    # subject -> predicate -> list of objects
    subject_pred_objs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj = t.subject
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = _clean(t.object)
        subject_pred_objs.setdefault(subj, {}).setdefault(pred, []).append(obj)

    # Build clauses per subject using aggregated objects.
    subject_clauses: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_pred_objs.items():
        for pred, objs in pred_objs.items():
            template = pred_map.get(pred, f"{pred} {{obj}}")
            joined_obj = _join_items(objs)
            clause = template.format(obj=joined_obj) if "{obj}" in template else template
            subject_clauses.setdefault(subj, []).append(clause)

    # Determine which subjects are only referenced as bands (objects) of associatedBand predicates.
    band_parents: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_pred_objs.items():
        for band in pred_objs.get("associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist", []):
            band_parents.setdefault(band, []).append(subj)

    # Helper to remove leading subject from a clause.
    def _strip_subject(clause: str, subj: str) -> str:
        prefix = f"{subj} "
        return clause[len(prefix) :] if clause.startswith(prefix) else clause

    sentences: list[str] = []
    # First, create sentences for subjects that are not only bands referenced elsewhere.
    for subj, pred_objs in subject_pred_objs.items():
        if subj in band_parents:
            # This subject will be embedded in its parent’s sentence.
            continue

        parts: list[str] = []

        # Process non‑associatedBand predicates.
        for pred, objs in pred_objs.items():
            if pred == "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist":
                continue
            template = pred_map.get(pred, f"{pred} {{obj}}")
            joined_obj = _join_items([_clean(o) for o in objs])
            parts.append(template.format(obj=joined_obj))

        # Process associatedBand objects, embedding their own clauses if available.
        for band in pred_objs.get("associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist", []):
            band_phrase = f"plays for the {band} band"
            if band in subject_clauses:
                inner_clauses = [_strip_subject(c, band) for c in subject_clauses[band]]
                if inner_clauses:
                    band_phrase += " which " + _join_items(inner_clauses)
            parts.append(band_phrase)

        # Build the sentence for this subject.
        if not parts:
            continue
        if len(parts) == 1:
            body = parts[0]
        else:
            body = ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1]
        sentence = f"{subj} {body}."
        sentences.append(sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:])

    # Finally, add sentences for any band subjects that were not embedded (no parent).
    for band, parents in band_parents.items():
        if band in subject_pred_objs:
            # Already handled via embedding; skip.
            continue
        # If a band appears only as a subject without a parent, render its clauses normally.
        clauses = subject_clauses.get(band, [])
        if not clauses:
            continue
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            body = clauses[0]
        else:
            body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
        sentence = f"{band} {body}."
        sentences.append(sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:])

    # Combine all sentences.

    # Combine sentences for different subjects with a space.
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
